leadership.background: Founder of Hounds Town USA since 2007 and CEO of affiliate Three Village since 2004.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings over two decades of brand-specific operational experience.

leadership.name: Jackie Bondanza
leadership.background: Founder since 2013, previously serving as CEO from April 2021 to December 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides deep historical knowledge of the brand's growth and operations.

leadership.whyItMatters: Brings extensive executive leadership in the pet care franchise industry.

leadership.name: Courtney Allison
leadership.background: Chief Franchise Officer since March 2026, with prior executive operations roles at Spavia and Sola Salon Studios.

leadership.name: JD Prager

leadership.name: Jeanna Novy
leadership.background: VP of Finance since December 2021, previously Controller for Factor Design Build.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages the financial planning and reporting for the franchise system.

extracted.conceptRationale: The concept is an interactive dog care facility offering daycare, boarding, and grooming services.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires active management and a trained team to handle dog safety, daycare, and boarding operations.

riskReasons: Pro forma is built on only 1 franchised location — a very thin sample; treat the modeled franchisee economics as low-confidence.
Capital gap of $644,882 is ~2.6x your liquid — meaningful outside financing required.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: Franchised Locations (Open 10+ Years) Average Net Revenue

source.label: Franchised Locations (Open 10+ Years) Average Net Revenue

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $894,882 mid-point build-out, there is a $644,882 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $77,968/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The concept is an interactive dog care facility offering daycare, boarding, and grooming services.

benchmark.label: General franchise (uncategorized)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Cost of goods and labor are not disclosed in any FDD — model them explicitly before trusting any margin figure.
benchmark.rampNote: Assume 6–18 months to a mature run-rate.
considerations: Budget COGS and labor explicitly — neither is in the FDD, and together they usually run 45–65% of sales.
Use the Item 20 franchisee list to validate real operating costs and ramp before committing capital.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 8–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 5.6–9.8 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “General franchise (uncategorized)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 6–12% category band.
Estimated at the 15–35% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
